key_risk_factors.explanation: Lifestyle Risk Index: 0.72/1.0 — multiple lifestyle risk factors contributing to elevated risk
ST depression: 2.4 — significant ST changes may indicate myocardial ischemia
Resting blood pressure 148 mmHg — elevated, suggesting arterial stress
Exercise-induced angina: Present — chest pain during exertion is a significant cardiac symptom
Cholesterol 267 mg/dl — high, associated with plaque formation

protective_factors.explanation: Maximum heart rate achieved: 112 bpm — reduced exercise capacity may indicate cardiac stress

recommendations: URGENT: Refer to cardiologist for comprehensive evaluation.
Order: Full lipid panel, HbA1c, ECG, and echocardiogram.
Initiate or review antihypertensive medication if BP ≥ 140/90.
Counsel on immediate lifestyle modifications: smoking cessation, diet, exercise.
Schedule follow-up cardiovascular risk assessment in 3 months.
Encourage patient to complete lifestyle modification questionnaire.
